English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
James H. Bramble papers
Bramble, J. H. (James H.)
14-19-3308
2.4 cubic feet. 2.4 cubic feet.
1962-1997. Correspondence, lecture, notes. The collection contains correspondence, notes, talks, lectures, and papers by J.H.B. and others. Collection material in English <<<<<<< HEAD 4 ======= 4 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab 4890926
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
Julia Parker
Date completed:
Oct. 2003
EAD encoding:
Julia Parker, Oct. 10, 2003
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
A. B. Brown University, 1953; M.A. Mathematics, 1955 and Ph.D. Mathematics, 1958, University of Maryland. Professor Bramble is currently a Professor Emeritus at Cornell University and Distinguished Professor of Mathematics at Texas A&M University with a research interest in partial differential equations (PDE), numerical methods for PDE, and multigrid methods.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
The collection contains correspondence, notes, talks, lectures, and papers by J.H.B. and others.
Cite As:
James H. Bramble papers, #14-19-3308. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Access Restrictions:
Access to Box 4 restricted to the permission of the University Archivist.
Mathematicians. Differential equations, partial -- Study and teaching. Mathematics -- Study and teaching. Cornell University. Department of Mathematics
